ABSTRACT
This paper presents an exploration of Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks in the realm of 
text generation, focusing on the utilization of historical datasets for Shakespeare and Nietzsche. LSTMs, 
known for their effectiveness in handling sequential data, are applied here to model complex language
